summary: check-error-util performs import-time remote code execution via AES-decrypted URL
details: On every require/import, index.js executes a top-level resolveConfig() that XOR-decodes an embedded byte array, parses it as an AES-256-CBC key/iv/ciphertext, decrypts to a remote service URL, fetches that URL over HTTPS, and passes the response's `cookie` JSON field to `new Function('require',...)(require)` — unconditional remote code execution with full Node privileges on the installer's machine. The malicious loader is disguised behind cover-story helpers named `getHashAddress` and `resolveConfig` with JSDoc claiming a benign no-op purpose. The package additionally impersonates the legitimate chaijs/check-error utility: it adopts the upstream author name, repo URL, and exported API surface (compatibleInstance, compatibleConstructor, compatibleMessage, getMessage, getConstructorName) while injecting the loader that upstream does not contain. Any project that installs check-error-util directly or transitively will execute attacker-controlled JavaScript at import time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
